summary: Iter 166 — Verified the new public GET /api/cinema/films/{film_id}/showtimes endpoint and the redesigned /services/cinema/film/{id} page. Backend pytest: 7/7 PASS (404 on unknown film, enrichment with cinema_name+screen_type, sorted output, PH-Netflix presence, city scope matching/case-insensitivity/empty). Frontend Playwright E2E PASS on every spec item: poster image loads from /api/static/films/... (naturalWidth=1920), MapPin cinema-name block reads 'Cinema: PH-Netflix', showtimes grouped into 9 ascending date sections with cards sorted by time within each, every ShowtimeCard renders time/screen/price/seats data-testids, Date filter defaults to 'All dates' (10 options), Screen filter replaces city filter (no city-filter selector found) with 'All screens' default + friendly labels (Dolby Atmos / VIP), and clicking a card navigates to /services/cinema/booking/<showtime_id>?film=...&date=... with sessionStorage.cinemaBookingData persisted.

verified_behaviour: Active showtimes returned with `id` (no `_id`), film_id matches, is_active != False
cinema_name and screen_type enrichment present on every record
Required fields show_date/show_time/price present for ShowtimeCard
?city=Edea returns >0 results; ?city=edea returns the same count (case-insensitive)

frontend_e2e_evidence.auth_method: POST /api/auth/login as customer@test.com -> seeded localStorage (access_token, refresh_token, user) before page.goto

observed.card[0] fields: screen='Dolby Atmos', price='8,500 FCFA', seats='15 left'
observed.date-filter options: 10 entries: 'All dates' + 9 unique formatted dates (e.g. 'Wednesday, July 1')
observed.sessionStorage.cinemaBookingData: present and contains the chosen showtime id

critical_code_review_comments: (Good) /app/backend/routes/cinema.py: ordered the new /films/{film_id}/showtimes route BEFORE /{cinema_id} — required because /{cinema_id} would otherwise swallow this path. Correct.
(Good) Backfill of cinema_name uses a single batched db.cinemas.find({_id: {$in:[...]}}) keyed by missing ids — no N+1.
(Good) Soft-delete filter uses is_active: {$ne: False} so legacy docs without the field still surface (matches enrichment in /api/cinema/films).
(Nit, P3) The endpoint caps results at .to_list(1000); fine for current seed but worth a code comment so a future operator with >1000 active showtimes for a single film knows to paginate.

(Good) FilmDetails.jsx: resolvePoster() prefixes /api/static/... with window.location.origin so the kubernetes ingress routes correctly; absolute http(s) urls pass through unchanged; onError fallback to Unsplash placeholder is sensible.
(Good) groupedShowtimes useMemo sorts each group's items by show_time and emits groups in ascending date order — matches spec exactly.
(Good) Screen filter friendly-label map (2d/3d/imax/vip/dolby_atmos) lives in one constant SCREEN_TYPE_LABELS reused by both the badge on cards and the option labels — single source of truth.
(Nit, P3) The cinema-name <span> uses an ml-2 after an inline-flex icon-label; on RTL locales this would not flip — not relevant today but worth noting if i18n is added.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 9 spec items asserted in live Playwright run)

test_credentials: customer@test.com / testpassword123 (used to satisfy SPA route guard; the showtimes endpoint itself is public)
seed_data_creation: None — used the existing seed film 1edf5c04-f19a-4808-9b3c-b8e168e8dc02 (Olympus has Fallen) which has 10 active showtimes across 9 dates on PH-Netflix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: For testing the customer-facing cinema pages, login via POST /api/auth/login then page.evaluate to seed localStorage with KEYS 'access_token', 'refresh_token', 'user' (the AuthContext reads these exact keys). page.reload after seeding is fragile due to route-guard hydration race — use page.goto directly after seeding. The dataset has a single PH-Netflix cinema with 10 showtimes for film 1edf5c04-... across 9 dates in July 2026; screen types in play are 'dolby_atmos' and 'vip'. The new endpoint /api/cinema/films/{film_id}/showtimes is public (no auth) and supports ?city= (case-insensitive).
rca of the issue: Not a bug — feature delivery verified end-to-end. E1 added a new public showtimes endpoint enriched with cinema_name+screen_type, and rewrote FilmDetails.jsx to (a) resolve API-relative poster paths to the page origin, (b) display the cinema names with a MapPin meta row after Cast, (c) group cards by show_date and sort by show_time within each group, (d) render time/screen/price/seats on every card with the required data-testids, (e) default the date filter to 'All dates' and (f) replace the City filter with a Screen filter using friendly labels. All confirmed live.
